title: ArtifactExplorer Concept frontmatter
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter of a concept file under concepts/<Name>.md. Each file defines one forensic concept that artifacts reference via references-data entries.

name.description: Concept identifier. Must match the filename without .md and match the concept name used in artifact references-data entries.

kind.description: identifier = globally-unique forensic key (useful as cross-artifact join). value-type = reusable non-unique value (paths, URLs, hashes — shared across artifacts but not identity-defining).

lifetime.description: Natural lifetime of instances of this concept. Permanent = resolves to a durable real-world entity. Persistent = stable across reboot but admin-mutable. Session-scoped = unique-per-boot. Runtime = unique-per-process.

description.description: Short prose description — what this concept represents and what makes it distinct.

link-affinity.description: Primary link-affinity for concept rendering. Same enum as artifact.link.

canonical-format.description: The canonical text form or structural notation for this concept (e.g., 'S-1-5-21-<...>' for UserSID, 'SHA-256 hex' for ExecutableHash).

roles.description: The semantic roles this concept can play when referenced by an artifact. The `id` is the role token used in references-data.

known-containers.description: Artifact names expected to reference this concept. Present purely as documentation — the build does not require these to exist.

provenance.description: Source attribution. Each item is either a source-registry ID (string) or an object {source, section?, note?}. Every ID must resolve to an entry in schema/sources.yaml.
